channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: He's one reason why aid cuts weren't as dire for the HIV population as predicted
item.description: Harerimana Ismail of Uganda is a community health worker who checks on kids with HIV. He lost his salary after the Trump administration's aid cuts but he keeps doing his job.
item.content:encoded:
Harerimana Ismail of Uganda is a community health worker who checks on kids with HIV. He lost his salary after the Trump administration's aid cuts but he keeps doing his job.

(Image credit: Ben de la Cruz/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Trump is dismantling democracy, reports find. And, Treasury to take over student loans
item.description: Recent studies show the U.S. is slipping further from democracy. And, the Trump administration plans to transfer federal student loans from the Education Department to the Treasury Department.
item.content:encoded:
Recent studies show the U.S. is slipping further from democracy. And, the Trump administration plans to transfer federal student loans from the Education Department to the Treasury Department.

(Image credit: Jim Watson)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Israel launches more strikes on Tehran as Iran continues attacks on Gulf oil facilities
item.description: The latest strikes come after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that Israel would "hold off on future attacks" on Iran's energy infrastructure, following Trump's request.
item.content:encoded:
The latest strikes come after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that Israel would "hold off on future attacks" on Iran's energy infrastructure, following Trump's request.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: From mall to torture site: The debate over El Helicoide's future in Venezuela
item.description: Once a futuristic shopping mall, El Helicoide became one of Venezuela's most feared prisons. Now, as the country changes, so does its fate — erase it, rebuild it, or remember what happened inside.
item.content:encoded:
Once a futuristic shopping mall, El Helicoide became one of Venezuela's most feared prisons. Now, as the country changes, so does its fate — erase it, rebuild it, or remember what happened inside.

(Image credit: Ariana Cubillos)
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda

item.title: What president had the lowest approval rating of the 20th century? The quiz knows
item.description: What could be more delightful than cannibal invertebrates and food-related weather events? A lot of things!
item.content:encoded: What could be more delightful than cannibal invertebrates and food-related weather events? A lot of things!
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: 10 tried-and-true methods to stay off your phone, according to our readers
item.description: We asked our audience to share the creative ways they limit their own phone use. They range from the practical (keep your phone in another room) to the creative (pair your phone with a fun paperback).
item.content:encoded: We asked our audience to share the creative ways they limit their own phone use. They range from the practical (keep your phone in another room) to the creative (pair your phone with a fun paperback).
item.dc:creator: Mika Ellison

item.title: An immigration court few have heard of is quietly shaping policy behind the scenes
item.description: President Trump has slashed the number of people on the Board of Immigration Appeals and stacked it with his appointees, tightening the due process available for immigrants, an NPR analysis shows.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has slashed the number of people on the Board of Immigration Appeals and stacked it with his appointees, tightening the due process available for immigrants, an NPR analysis shows.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Why it's so hard for world leaders to bring down oil and gasoline prices
item.description: From waiving the Jones Act to rerouting oil through the Red Sea, governments are doing their best to make up for the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, but prices are still rising.
item.content:encoded:
From waiving the Jones Act to rerouting oil through the Red Sea, governments are doing their best to make up for the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, but prices are still rising.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: FCC approves the merger of local television owners Nexstar and Tegna
item.description: The FCC has approved the sale of Tegna television stations to rival Nexstar Media Group Thursday. The deal would create a company that owns 259 television stations in 44 states.
item.content:encoded: The FCC has approved the sale of Tegna television stations to rival Nexstar Media Group Thursday. The deal would create a company that owns 259 television stations in 44 states.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Pittsburgh synagogue attack survivors talk about their friendship and healing journey
item.description: For StoryCorps, two survivors of the 2018 Synagogue attack in Pittsburgh talk about their friendship.
item.content:encoded: For StoryCorps, two survivors of the 2018 Synagogue attack in Pittsburgh talk about their friendship.
item.dc:creator: Kerrie Hillman
